summary: LoRA adds low-rank adapters on top of transformers-style modules.

selected_base_repo.evidence: Paper and repository both reference the Transformers codebase.

base_repo_candidates.evidence: Paper and repository both reference the Transformers codebase.

contributions.title: Low-rank adaptation modules
impl_hints: Insert trainable rank-decomposition matrices into attention projections.

contributions.title: Frozen backbone fine-tuning
impl_hints: Keep pretrained weights frozen and only optimize LoRA parameters.

diff_clusters.label: LoRA adapter modules
diff_clusters.summary: Adds reusable low-rank adapter layers and wiring into linear projections.

code_anchors.snippet:
class LoraLinear(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_features, out_features, r=8, alpha=16):
        super().__init__()
        self.base_layer = nn.Linear(in_features, out_features, bias=False)
        self.lora_A = nn.Parameter(torch.zeros(r, in_features))
        self.lora_B = nn.Parameter(torch.zeros(out_features, r))
        self.scaling = alpha / r
code_anchors.reason: Introduces the trainable low-rank A/B matrices described in the LoRA paper.
Wires the new adapter layer into target attention projections.

diff_clusters.label: Training freeze logic
diff_clusters.summary: Restricts optimizer updates to adapter parameters while freezing the base model.

code_anchors.reason: Keeps the pretrained backbone frozen and updates only LoRA parameters.

mappings.evidence: The adapter modules implement the rank-decomposition described in the paper.
The training utilities freeze pretrained weights and keep LoRA weights trainable.
